text: At roughly {{your_monthly_expenses}} a month in spending, your {{your_cash}} in cash is about {{coverage_days}} days of runway before anything else breaks your fall.

If unemployment replaced about 45% of prior pay, the monthly hole after losing {{your_monthly_income}} in take-home looks roughly like {{income_gap}} before you touch the credit card at {{your_credit_card_debt}}.
With rent near {{your_monthly_rent}}, the Fed at {{fed_funds_rate}}, and inflation near {{inflation_rate}}, every "small" APR or lease escalation shows up in the same checking account.
A {{your_credit_score}} score and {{your_credit_card_debt}} in revolving debt change the interest rate table you actually meet in the wild, not the headline teaser on a website.
Reports near {{sp500_level}} on the S&P are abstract until you compare them to {{your_investment_value}} actually invested on your statement — the gap is the lesson.
Net worth near {{your_net_worth}} in the demographic seed is one line: assets minus liabilities. Replace every number with yours and the sentence still has to balance.
Tax day is a document day, not a panic day, if {{marginal_tax_rate}} and your real withholding already live on paper.
